data.content:
The file is generated successfully (`obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`), but it's never added to the `Compile` item group, so the compiler doesn't see it.

The fix is to add a `<Compile Include="..." />` after generating the file:

arguments.new_str:
<!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates a `.cs` file into the intermediate output directory, but it never adds it to the `Compile` item group. The target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"`, which means it runs before compilation, but the generated file isn't included in the compilation items.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group after writing it. I need to add a `<Compile Include="..." />` item in the target.

arguments.new_str:
<!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />"}

data.content:
Build succeeds and runs correctly now.

**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote the `.cs` file to the intermediate output directory but never added it to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild generated the file, then the compiler had no idea it existed.

**Fix:** Added `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` inside the target so the generated file is included in compilation.
